title: Retention policy drift — system config vs policy catalogue
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate execution of retention (covered by 22.49.1).
evidence: Saved search 'retention_policy_drift' (daily) compares each system's configured retention against the authoritative policy catalogue.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Storage-limitation principle enforced at the system configuration layer.
CPRA data-minimisation and retention disclosure align.
Derived from GDPR@2016/679 Art.5(1)(e) (parent assurance='full'). UK GDPR preserves parent clause numbering; inherited assurance degraded one step.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An S3 bucket with data_domain=customer has a lifecycle of 7 years but the policy catalogue requires 3 years; the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Bucket lifecycle matches catalogue; no alert.

spl:
index=datastore sourcetype=retention:config earliest=-1d
| lookup retention_policy.csv data_domain OUTPUT policy_days
| eval drift_days=abs(configured_days-policy_days)
| where drift_days>0
| table store data_domain configured_days policy_days drift_days
description: Ensures system-level retention configuration stays aligned to the authoritative policy catalogue.
value: Turns retention policy adherence into a continuously-measured metric per system.
implementation: (1) Export retention configuration nightly; (2) maintain retention_policy.csv; (3) run daily.
visualization: Table of drift stores, bar chart of drift magnitude by data-domain, single-value 'stores aligned to policy'.
knownFalsePositives: Legal-hold flagged systems legitimately have longer retention; exclude via hold_in_force=true.
